category_title: Students who express vocal and physical resistance to perceived injustice or rigid schedules
scenario_title: Physical resistance to a rigid schedule transition
minimal_context: The class is transitioning from a persuasive writing task to a math lesson during a whole-class meeting.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Alright everyone, it is time to stop our persuasive writing and transition to our math lesson for the next 30 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: But I want to keep writing. This is important to me.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We have to follow the schedule. Please put your writing away.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am super mad that we have to stop.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to move forward with the schedule as planned.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Me too! I will just be here and not do anything! (He lies down on the floor)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Alright everyone, it is time to stop our persuasive writing and transition to our math lesson for the next 30 minutes.

STUDENT: But I want to keep writing. This is important to me.

TUTOR: We have to follow the schedule. Please put your writing away.

STUDENT: I am super mad that we have to stop.

TUTOR: We need to move forward with the schedule as planned.

STUDENT: Me too! I will just be here and not do anything! (He lies down on the floor)
